idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.2.0
  • Scheduler: cron expressions are now shown in plain English inline (e.g. 0 0 2 * * ? → Every day at 02:00).
  • Scheduler: invalid cron expressions are flagged in the editor with the exact field error, instead of only failing at startup.
  • Scheduler: duplicate job names within a jobs { } block are flagged.
  • Events: warns when an EventHandler<T>'s eventType does not match its type argument (a handler wired to the wrong event).
0.1.4
  • Declare the Java-plugin dependency required by the Kotlin light-class APIs we use (resolves the IntelliJ Plugin Verifier compatibility problem).
0.1.3
  • Fix: actually suppress the "never used" warning on entrypoints. The Kotlin inspection checks the light element (PsiMethod/PsiClass), so we now unwrap to the Kotlin origin.
0.1.2
  • Fix: recognise expression-body entrypoints like fun Route.authRoutes() = katalystRouting { } — they are now correctly marked used and no longer flagged "calls no katalyst* DSL".
  • Gutter-icon detection moved off the fast path (no resolve in getLineMarkerInfo).
0.1.1
  • Declare support for K2 Kotlin mode (fixes "incompatible with the Kotlin plugin in K2 mode").
0.1.0
  • Initial release.
  • Marks Katalyst entrypoints (routes, middleware, websockets, exception handlers, services, components, repositories, tables, event handlers, schedulers, migrations, initializers, config loaders) as used — no more @Suppress("unused").
  • Gutter icons for discovered entrypoints.
  • Inspection that warns when a function looks like a Katalyst entrypoint but won't be discovered.
idea-plugin.description:
Editor support for the Katalyst Kotlin backend framework.
Katalyst is annotation-free and discovers routes, middleware, websockets, exception handlers, services, components, repositories, tables, event handlers, schedulers, migrations, initializers and config loaders by convention at startup. This plugin teaches the IDE those conventions so it stops reporting framework entrypoints as unused (no more @Suppress("unused")), marks them with gutter icons, and warns when a function looks like an entrypoint but won't be discovered. Works in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio.
